# Onbuilding — single source of truth (Movemental)

**Status:** Canon as of 2026-04-23  
**Replaces:** Informal "residency" usage, 12-week-as-onboarding, and conflated "weeks 1–4" playbooks.  
**Authority:** This document is decisive. If another file in the repo disagrees, **this document wins** until the file is updated.

**Purpose:** Define the **one** onbuilding model so product, services, and narrative are buildable, teachable, and shippable without interpretation drift.

All prior ambiguity about "which four weeks" or "onboarding length" is **resolved here**, not diffused.

---

## 1. The onbuilding model (definitive)

### What onbuilding is

- **The only structured, cohort-based build** every committed leader goes through to turn a **fully provisioned** (template-based) platform into a **launched, navigable, formation-ready** presence.
- **Duration:** always **4 weeks**. No variants.
- **Format:** always **cohort-based, group experience**. Peers, shared rhythm, and scheduled cohort time are the **primary** accountability. One-to-one is **ancillary** (e.g. brief office hours), not the spine.
- **Prerequisites:** the leader has **already said yes** (contract / commitment) and completed **pre-onboarding** (identity, access, content samples, platform shell live on a design/template — *not* a blank repo).
- **Build focus (non-negotiable outcomes):** evergreen **articles** (at minimum **one pillar + one cluster**; strategy map that is **sufficient to launch**, not an exhaustive content farm), at least **one** structured **pathway**, at least **one** **course** (complete enough to be **usable** by a real learner, or with one module in publishable form and the rest specified — *never* a vague “we’ll do the course later”).
- **Intelligence layer:** **AI ingestion** of the leader’s body of work, **theme** and structure extraction, **voice/style** alignment, and **very high quality drafts**; humans **lead**, **decide**, **edit**, and **sign**.
- **System design goals:** only do what is **necessary**; **remove decision ambiguity** (stepwise); **default against overproduction and blank-page work**; **clarity & launch over perfect completeness**.

### What onbuilding is not

- It is **not** open-ended "enablement," ad hoc office hours, or a library of best-effort content.
- It is **not** a **12-week** (or 8-week) "onboarding" program. Those labels describe **time after** onboarding if we offer support — **not** the build itself.
- It is **not** the same as **onboarding** (pre-cohort: contract, access, **provisioned** Next/React tenant, template, ingest kickoff, prerequisites complete).

### Onboarding (pre-cohort only) — for contrast

- **Onboarding** = everything **before** week 1 of the cohort: agreement, **template-based** platform stand-up, design selection, **corpus** handoff for ingest, access to AI tools, pre-reading, pre-onboarding completed.
- Onboarding does **not** require the leader to "finish" the public site. It **does** require the **starting line** to be a **real product surface**, not an empty project.

### Long-term usage (after week 4)

- **Ongoing** publishing, audience growth, monetization experiments, and optional **stabilization** or coaching windows — **separate** SKUs, **separate** language, **not** a longer onbuilding.

---

## 2. The 4-week structure (FINAL)

Cohort = **one scheduled session per week (90 minutes, facilitator-led)** + **async** daily work. Async follows the **same weekly arc**; daily detail lives in the published cohort playbook (`docs/articles/07-author-onboarding-course-outline.md`) but **must** stay consistent with the table below.

### Week 1 — Foundation: voice, themes, and architecture

| Dimension | Definition |
| --------- | ---------- |
| **Objective** | Name what must be true for this platform to *form* people, not just host files. **Voice and watermark** (what belongs / what does not), **3–5 pathway candidates**, **corpus** positioned as one library, **monetization intent** (what is free vs. gated). **No** unfilled "we’ll pick themes later." |
| **Leader actions** | Contribute **source** material; write watermark + pathway candidates; complete voice **calibration** exercise; one-page **monetization** map. |
| **AI / system actions** | Ingest and chunk corpus; **surface** themes, gaps, and overlaps; **draft** voice memos and candidate pathway blurbs for human edit; pre-fill **structural** suggestions (not published copy without sign-off). |
| **Cohort interaction** | Present **watermark**, **pathways**, and **map**; peers challenge vagueness; facilitator enforces "enough to proceed." |
| **Outputs** | Watermark; pathway candidates; voice memo; **monetization** map; cohort peer feedback log. |

### Week 2 — Articles: pillar + cluster (launch-grade)

| Dimension | Definition |
| --------- | ---------- |
| **Objective** | **One pillar** and **one cluster** evergreen article **shipped to production** (or equivalent production-equivalent for that tenant) that are **on-brand**, linked, and **RAG-** and **SEO-sensible**. **Strategic** article map (pillars + planned clusters) **sufficient to launch** — *not* every future article written. |
| **Leader actions** | Own **editing** and **sign-off** on drafts; **reject** generic tone; set **one** execution slice (which pillar first). |
| **AI / system actions** | Topic cluster suggestions; **drafts** in nine-section architecture; **internal** link graph suggestions; **voice** audit list. |
| **Cohort interaction** | **Peer review** of pillars (rubric: nine sections, five voice markers) — no vague praise. |
| **Outputs** | **Published** pillar + cluster; **strategic** inventory table; **execution** slice for the primary pathway. |

### Week 3 — Course: usable arc + one deep week

| Dimension | Definition |
| --------- | ---------- |
| **Objective** | **Transformatively scoped** 8-week **learner** course **specified**; **at least** one week (e.g. week 2) **fully drafted** for use (teaching + case + action + AI prompts for dissonance/action/reflection as applicable). **Thesis** clear. **Assessment** optional concept only. |
| **Leader actions** | **Write/revise** the “load-bearing” module; set **dissonance** stakes for the arc; sign off. |
| **AI / system actions** | Scaffold from corpus; **draft** modules; help with **assessment** hooks (optional). |
| **Cohort interaction** | **Outline** read-through; "Is the action step **doable** in real life this week?" |
| **Outputs** | 8-week outline; **one** week **publishable**; dissonance prompts (where used); course entry wired in nav when applicable. |

### Week 4 — Pathway + integration + launch

| Dimension | Definition |
| --------- | ---------- |
| **Objective** | **One** primary **pathway** (12-section model or approved equivalent) that **wires** pillar, cluster, course, and (if any) assessment. **Ecosystem map** + **launch** brief. **Go-live** to **real** users. |
| **Leader actions** | **Draft/approve** pathway sections; **click-through** the live tenant; sign **launch** checklist. |
| **AI / system actions** | Suggest **links**, **curated resources** block, and **AI Lab** copy; last-mile **ingest** for published URLs. |
| **Cohort interaction** | **Commissioning**: read hooks aloud; **accountability** pairs for 30-day rhythm. |
| **Outputs** | **Live** platform with: **pathway** + **article layer** (minimum) + **course** (usable) + **AI** aligned to **voice** + **90-day** post-launch plan (clarity, not more volume). |

---

## 3. Core outputs (required) — what is "enough"

| Output | "Enough" to launch | Not required (post-launch OK) |
| ------ | ------------------ | ----------------------------- |
| **Articles** | **One pillar + one cluster** in production, **on voice**, interlinked. **Thematic map** of future pillars/clusters (strategy table) — *not* dozens of live articles. | Filling the entire 60+ article reference map. |
| **Pathway** | **At least one** complete pathway to the **12-section** (or product-approved) standard that **routes** to the course + key articles. | A pathway for *every* theme. |
| **Course** | **8-week** arc **designed** + **one** week in **teachable** shape. Remaining weeks may be **specified** with drafts pending **only if** the launch plan names dates. A **viable** first cohort or async path exists. | Every module filmed or fully written. |
| **AI / voice** | Ingested corpus, **voice markers**, **RAG** where product supports it, **signed** copy only on public pages. | Exhaustive custom agents for every sub-task. |
| **Platform** | **Template** tenant **fully** provisioned **before** day 1 of the cohort. | Custom redesign of the whole template mid-build. |

**Principle:** **Launch > perfection.** The bar is **integrity + clarity + real navigation**, not **volume**.

---

## 4. The workflow (exact)

**Flow:** `corpus → ingest → theme extraction → structural map (pillars, pathway, course arc) → AI-assisted drafts → human edit & sign → publish & wire pathway`

| Stage | Automated | Human | Optional |
| ----- | --------- | ----- | -------- |
| Ingest, chunk, embed | Yes | Curate what to exclude, legal/sensitivity | — |
| Theme / pillar ideas | Suggest | **Approve** and **prune** | — |
| Drafts | **High** quality first draft | **Every** public line **earned** by the leader’s judgment | Stylistic rewrites for tone |
| Voice guard / audit | Assist | **Overrule**; fix edge cases | — |
| Publish | Pipeline | **Sign-off** on go-live | — |
| Cohort | Scheduling nudges | **Facilitator** sets pace and **kills** scope creep | 1:1 for blockers (short) |

**Rule:** **Draft-heavy, not blank-page-heavy.** The leader’s time goes to **decision, deletion, and distinction** — not staring at a cursor.

---

## 5. Product model

- **Starting state:** **Provisioned** Next.js / React **template** (Movemental **spine**): auth where applicable, article routes, **course** shell, **pathway** shell, **AI** integration points, brand tokens applied.
- **Pre-built** before onbuilding: **design**, **empty** shells of content types, **ingest** pipeline, **not** a **blank** repo.
- **Built during the 4 weeks:** **the minimum** that makes the platform *about this leader* — not every feature, not every page.
- **AI agents / assistants:** Ingest, outline, **draft**, audit for voice, suggest structure; they **do not** replace **human** byline, **governance** choice, or **theological** judgment.

---

## 6. Cohort model

- **Cadence:** **4 weeks**; **1× weekly** cohort (90 min) + **async** work between (expect **5–8 hrs/week** leader time; adjust per cohort agreement — **one** number published to cohort, not ad hoc per person).
- **Peers:** **Accountability** on shipped work, **peers** challenge fuzzy strategy; **not** social hour only.
- **Facilitator:** **Sets** the edge of "done," **prevents** scope tripling, **runs** rubric-based **review**, **keeps** time to **launch**.
- **1:1:** **Not** the primary locus. **Office hours** or **short** unblockers only.
- **Engagement expectation:** If you can’t do the **weekly** cohort, you’re in the **wrong** cohort; reschedule into the **next** slot — *do not* silently stretch a "12-week" shadow build.

---

## 7. Constraints and principles

- **No** blank-page heroics — start from **drafts and maps**.
- **No** content for its own sake — if it does not **serve** pathway, course, or voice, it waits.
- **No** overproduction in the 4 weeks — we **refuse** busyness as a stand-in for clarity.
- **Clarity over exhaustiveness.**
- **Launch > perfection.**
- **Cohort** exists for **momentum, clarity, accountability, peer reinforcement** — the same four goals every week in different form.

---

## 8. Naming standard

| Term | Meaning |
| ---- | ------- |
| **Onbuilding** | The **4-week, cohort-based** build. **Primary** public and internal name for the phase. |
| **Onboarding** | **Pre-cohort** only: access, **provisioned** platform, prerequisites, handoff to **onbuilding** week 1. |
| **Residency** | **Deprecated** as a *program name* for this phase. In historical or book prose, if used, map → **"onbuilding (cohort)"** in a footnote or one-line parenthetical. Do not introduce *new* "residency" in product. |
| **"12-week onboarding"** | **Prohibited** to mean the build. Use: **"post-launch stabilization"** or **"coaching window (optional)"** with **explicit** dates if we sell it. |
| **"2–4 week launch"** (marketing) | If used, must be unpacked as: **pre-onboarding** platform provision + **4-week** onbuilding **=** public launch, **or** the sentence must be **removed**. Never imply the **entire** intellectual build is 24 hours. |

---

## 9. Product gaps revealed

What must **exist in product** to be honest to this model:

- **Onboarding → onbuilding transition:** a **gated** checklist (corpus, legal, access, **template** live) before **cohort** start date.
- **Cohort UI:** rosters, **session** times, **submission** of pillar/cluster for **peer** review, facilitator **visibility** on progress.
- **AI drafting system:** RAG on **tenant** corpus, **draft** with **citation** to source chunks, **no** unapproved publish.
- **Pathway and course builder:** 12-section pathway pattern; course week pattern; **linking** to articles; **one** end-to-end **click** from pathway → content.
- **Progress tracking:** "Week 1–4" for **onbuilding** only, **separate** from long-term product analytics.
- **Launch** toggle or checklist: **readiness** for public URL (even if "soft" launch to network).

---

## 10. Required repo changes (conflicts and corrections)

The following **six files** were misaligned. Each row: **conflict** → **why it was wrong** → **remediation** (this PR applies the corrected text; summary here).

| File | Conflict | Why wrong | Remediation |
| ---- | -------- | --------- | ------------ |
| `docs/articles/07-author-onboarding-course-outline.md` | Titled "Author Residency," implied optional solo pace, "staging" publish, success = exhaustive map | Onbuilding is **one** model, **cohort-first**, **production** launch, "enough" not infinite map | Retitled to **Onbuilding**; **canonical** block + cohort-first language; **production**; outcomes tied to **SSOT**; "residency" deprecated |
| `docs/system-builds/charters/04-content.md` | "Pathways per **theme**" and **every** theme article wave in 4 weeks | **Overproduces** vs. **minimum launch**; org builds still **one** cohort model | Aligned to **MVP** outputs + **cohort**; exit matches **onbuilding**; adoption milestones **unchanged** in spirit |
| `docs/business-docs/core-docs/12-author-journey-map.md` | "Phase 2 = Weeks 1-4" as generic onboarding; **8/12 week** onboarding | Conflated **onboarding** length with **onbuilding**; implied **1:1** weekly calls as spine | **Phases** split: pre-onboarding → **onbuilding (4 weeks)** → post-launch; **tiers** = support **after** onbuilding, not **longer** build |
| `docs/business-docs/core-docs/27-post-commitment-onboarding.md` | **12 weeks** = "onboarding" | **Violates** single 4-week build; **1:1**-heavy | Restructured: pre-on → **4-week onbuilding** (cohort) → **stabilization** optional; **1:1** de-emphasized for onbuilding |
| `docs/arguments/custom-gpt/messaging-04-strategy.md` | "12-week onboarding" | Falsely teaches duration | Replaced with **4-week** onbuilding + pre-on + optional post support |
| `docs/arguments/custom-gpt/messaging-08-by-page.md` | Repeated 12-week block; "2-4 week" = ambiguous | Same | **4-week** onbuilding; **2–4** = template **ops** speed vs. **4-week** **cohort** (disambiguate) |
| `docs/system-builds/charters/README.md` | "Residency" / "every theme" | Wrong naming / overproduction | Aligned to **onbuilding** + **SSOT**; **MVP** exit |
| `docs/business-docs/core-docs/29-the-execution-path.md` | 12-week onboarding in story beat | User-facing lie vs. product | Replaced with **pre-on** + **4-week** onbuilding + optional support |
| `docs/business-docs/documentation-index/27-post-commitment-onboarding.md` | 12-week timeline | Stale index | Pointer to **core** `27` + SSOT frame |

`docs/build/notes/onbuilding-4-week-course-current-understanding.md` (prior research note) is **not** the SSOT. **This file** is the only **definitive** onbuilding spec.

---

## 11. Buildability check (final)

- **One** interpretation: **4 weeks, cohort, after pre-onboarding, not 12-week onboarding.**
- **Outcomes** are **measurable** from **artifacts** in the week table.
- **No** copy in **aligned** repo materials should **teach** a different length or a **1:1-first** onbuilding; **messaging-04/08, 07, 04-content, 12, 27** are the **highest** **leverage** fixes.
- This document is the **test**: if a feature, deck, or script cannot trace to a section here, it does not ship under the onbuilding name.

---

*For day-by-day tasks within each week, use* `docs/articles/07-author-onboarding-course-outline.md` *as the operating playbook. In any conflict, §1–7 of this SSOT win, especially the “enough to launch” rules in §3.*
